subtitle_closing_a_port_prevents_f.en: Closing a port prevents further messages. The other port can still send, but messages are silently dropped.

subtitle_ports_are_transferable_on.en: Ports are transferable. Once transferred, the original reference becomes neutered (unusable).

subtitle_messageport_postmessage_u.en: MessagePort.postMessage uses the structured clone algorithm, supporting complex data types beyond JSON. Transferable objects can be moved (zero-copy) for performance.

subtitle_transferring_an_arraybuff.en: Transferring an ArrayBuffer moves it (zero-copy, original becomes detached). Cloning copies the data.

subtitle_check_the_cache_first_if.en: Check the cache first. If a cached response exists, serve it immediately. Only go to the network if the resource isn't in the cache. This is ideal for assets that rarely change — fonts, images, CSS/JS with versioned URLs.

subtitle_always_try_the_network_fi.en: Always try the network first. If the network responds, cache the result and serve it. If the network fails (offline, timeout), fall back to the cached version. Best for content that needs to be up-to-date — API responses, news feeds, user profiles.

subtitle_serve_the_cached_version.en: Serve the cached version immediately (fast!), but simultaneously fetch the latest version from the network and update the cache in the background. The user sees stale content now but gets fresh content on the next visit. Great for content that updates but isn't time-critical.

subtitle_only_serve_from_the_cache.en: Only serve from the cache. Never go to the network for this request. The resources must have been pre-cached during the install phase. This is the simplest strategy and guarantees offline availability for known assets.

subtitle_always_go_to_the_network.en: Always go to the network. Never use cache. This is equivalent to having no Service Worker for this request, but you can still add logging, analytics, or request modification. Use for requests that must always be live — login, checkout, real-time data.

subtitle_a_service_worker_goes_thr.en: A Service Worker goes through a specific lifecycle: registration → installation → waiting → activation. Understanding this lifecycle is crucial for handling updates correctly and avoiding unexpected behavior.
